source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Uncontrolled release of mechanical energy resulting in hand entrapment and amputation
actions_taken: Immediate first aid provided to control bleeding
Severed finger retrieved for possible reattachment
Injured person transferred to A platform for medical stabilization
Airlifted via helicopter to RLC base in Venice, LA
Transported by ambulance to University Medical Center New Orleans
All operations halted for the day
Slings subsequently procured for future operations
event.summary: On 30 July 2025 at 13:10 CDT, a Supreme Services crew contracted by W&T Offshore was laying down a 12-inch mechanical casing cutter from Well C005 into a shipping basket on South Pass 49 C platform. While guiding the tool into the basket, the outermost casing bowl (approximately 250 pounds) unexpectedly separated and slid down the tool due to a 'grease lock' created by heavy buildup of grease and debris from prior cutting operations. A Supreme operator's left hand was caught between the bowl and tool, resulting in amputation of one finger and traumatic crush injuries to two others. The operator was evacuated and underwent medical treatment resulting in permanent dismemberment of three fingers.
recommendations: Implement and enforce established 'no-hands' policy across all crew members
Provide hands-free tools and approved safety devices (push poles) prior to operations
Conduct task-specific pre-job safety meetings and risk assessments
Implement permit process authorizing manual tool handling
Mechanically restrain heavy components prior to orientation changes
Improve hazard recognition during rig down activities
Enhance crew training on hazard identification and procedural compliance
Establish stop work intervention protocols for hazardous hand positioning

hazards.name: Mechanical energy stored in casing cutter assembly
Heavy rotating/sliding components (250 lb outer bowl)
Grease and debris accumulation on mechanical interfaces

threats.name: Unexpected separation of outer bowl from casing cutter
Uncontrolled sliding of heavy bowl past stabilizer fins
Hand placement in pinch point during tool guidance

consequences.name: Hand entrapment between bowl and tool
Amputation of finger and crush injuries to hand
Loss of work capability and permanent disability

controls.name: No-hands policy
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent manual handling

supporting_text: Supreme maintained a formal 'no hands' policy (Dated: 11/30/2022), the policy was not effectively communicated to or enforced among the crew
crew members interviewed on location were either unaware of the policy or unfamiliar with its requirements
Apart from one recently hired employee whose knowledge was derived solely from the employee handbook during onboarding

controls.name: Hands-free tools and safety devices
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to eliminate manual handling
controls.lod_basis: Industry best practice for tool handling

supporting_text: hands-free tools were not provided until after the incident
The absence of push poles or other approved hands-free safety devices increased the likelihood of manual handling
the crew subsequently procured slings for use in future operations

controls.name: Task-specific pre-job safety meeting and risk assessment
controls.lod_basis: Standard safety practice for hazard identification

supporting_text: The crew relied on an unsupported assumption that the bowls would remain attached while being lifted from the casing jack to the shipping basket, with the potential hazard not identified due to the absence of a task-specific pre-job safety meeting or risk assessment

Insufficient hazard recognition during rig down: The potential for stored mechanical energy and the ability for the bowls to separate was not identified during pre-task hazard analysis or communicated in the Job Safety Analysis (JSA)

controls.name: Mechanical restraint of heavy components
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent uncontrolled movement

supporting_text: Inadequate securing of heavy components: The outermost bowl was not mechanically restrained prior to changing the cutter's orientation (moving it from the vertical position to the horizontal position in the basket), allowing it to slide under gravity when separation occurred
the crew subsequently procured slings for use in future operations

controls.name: Permit process for manual tool handling
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to authorize and control manual handling

supporting_text: the permit process authorizing manual tool handling was not implemented

controls.name: Cleaning and inspection of mechanical interfaces
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent grease lock condition

supporting_text: Subsequent inspection determined that the two bowls had been bonded together by a heavy buildup of grease and debris from prior cutting operations. This created what is commonly referred to as a 'grease lock'

controls.name: Stop work intervention and hazard oversight
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to halt unsafe work

supporting_text: No stop work intervention: The hazardous hand positioning was not corrected prior to movement, indicating an opportunity for improved hazard oversight

controls.name: Crew training on hazard identification and policy compliance

supporting_text: Crew familiarity and procedural drift: Routine handling of similar assemblies and lack of training may have contributed to complacency and deviation from the service company's 'no hands' policy

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
